mechanism_narrative: A declining FEDFUNDS rate lowers discount rates and eases financial conditions, which lifts equity valuations and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days when the policy rate is already near the zero lower bound.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not higher than baseline during periods of falling FEDFUNDS, or if the signal's rolling 2-year Rank IC stays below 0.03 after 2011-04-15.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above 8.5% signals persistent labor-market slack, weaker household income growth, and slower earnings expectations, which should depress S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations with UNRATE above 8.5% do not show negative average 20-day SPX returns, or if positive returns occur in more than 60% of triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous improvement in PAYEMS and RSAFS indicates broad-based job growth and stronger retail demand, which increases revenue expectations and supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS and RSAFS both remained below later-revised levels in the March 2011 snapshot
failure_condition: Reject if joint positive changes in PAYEMS and RSAFS do not outperform either series alone in predicting 60-day forward returns, or if the interaction term is not significant in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A broad rise in INDPRO above its recent level signals stronger industrial output and capacity utilization, which improves cyclical earnings expectations and should produce positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises above its 3-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if periods with INDPRO above its 3-month moving average do not show above-baseline 20-day SPX returns, or if the conditional hit rate falls below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: A surge in HOUST indicates accelerating residential investment and stronger downstream demand for materials, labor, and financial services, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the following 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST growth above 10% year-over-year is not followed by positive average 60-day SPX returns, or if the signal loses significance after controlling for baseline trend.

mechanism_narrative: When CPI inflation accelerates while FEDFUNDS remains anchored near zero, real rates fall and the policy stance turns easier in real terms, which should support positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL vintage value was 223.49 on 2011-03-01 and FEDFUNDS was 0.14%
failure_condition: Reject if rising CPIAUCSL with FEDFUNDS near zero does not outperform either proxy alone, or if positive 20-day returns do not cluster after real-rate compression episodes.

mechanism_narrative: A worsening labor market, defined by PAYEMS declining while UNRATE rises, indicates falling income and confidence, which should generate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as equities reprice near-term growth risk.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines for 2 consecutive months and UNRATE rises
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS vintage value was 130738.0 and UNRATE was 8.8 on 2011-03-01
failure_condition: Reject if combined PAYEMS declines and UNRATE increases fail to predict negative 5-day returns, or if triggered episodes show positive average returns more than 50% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: Weak quarterly GDPC1 growth indicates slowing aggregate demand and lower expected earnings growth, which should pressure S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days when the economy is still far below pre-crisis trend.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarterly growth falls below 1.5% annualized
failure_condition: Reject if quarters with GDPC1 growth below 1.5% annualized do not precede negative average 60-day SPX returns, or if the sign reverses in more than one rolling 3-year window.
